title: Method for preparing an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles
abstract: A method for preparing an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles is disclosed. The method comprises the step of performing phase transfer of a plurality of metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on a surface there of by contacting the metal oxide particles with a combination of tertiary amine and water to form a biphasic mixture, and agitating said biphasic mixture to produce an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands and tertiary amine ligands on the surface thereof.
claims: Claims The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A method for preparing an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles, comprising the step of: performing phase transfer of a plurality of metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on a surface there of by contacting the metal oxide particles with a combination of tertiary amine and water to form a biphasic mixture, and agitating said biphasic mixture to produce an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands and tertiary amine ligands on the surface thereof. 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the biphasic mixture is agitated by sonicating the biphasic mixture for greater than 5 minutes. 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands and tertiary amine ligands on the surface there of migrate from the biphasic mixture into the aqueous phase upon standing overnight. 4. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein prior to the phase transfer step, the method further comprises the step of: dissolving the tertiary amine in a solvent to form a solution of the tertiary amine. 5. A method according to claim 4, wherein the solvent is a non-polar solvent selected from the group consisting of hexane, toluene and a combination thereof. 6. A method according to claim 4, wherein the solvent is a polar solvent selected from the group consisting of water, methanol, ethanol, propanol, an ether, chloroform, dimethyl formamide (DMF), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), and any combination thereof. 7. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, further comprising the step of: separating the metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands and tertiary amine ligands on the surface thereof from the aqueous dispersion using a physical separation procedure. 8. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands and tertiary amine ligands are separated from the biphasic mixture by centrifuging the biphasic mixture at greater than 1000 g for greater than 2 minutes. 9. A method according to claim 1 , wherein prior to the phase transfer step, the method further comprises the step of: dispersing a plurality of metal oxide particles capped with tertiary amine ligands on a surface thereof in an organic solution of hydrophobic ligands to form a mixture, and agitating said mixture to form a plurality of metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on the surface thereof. 10. A method according to claim 9, wherein the hydrophobic ligands are dissolved in an organic solvent selected from the group consisting of acetone, methanol, ethanol, propanol, an ether, chloroform, dimethyl formamide (DMF), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), and any combination thereof. 11. A method according to claim 9 or 10, further comprising the step of: separating the metal oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on the surface thereof from the organic dispersion using a physical separation procedure. 12. A method according to claim 7 or 11 , wherein the physical separation procedure is selected from the group consisting of magnetic separation, centrifugation, filtration and decantation. 13. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the plurality of metal oxide particles are iron oxide particles, and prior to the phase transfer step of, the method further comprises the step of: contacting an aqueous solution of iron (III) and iron (II) ions with a tertiary amine and hydrophobic ligands dissolved in an organic solvent to facilitate hydrolysis of the iron (III) and iron (II) ions to produce an organic dispersion of iron oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on the surface thereof. 14. A method according to claim 13, wherein the iron (III) and iron (II) ions are present in the aqueous solution in a 2:1 molar ratio. 15. A method according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the iron (III) ions are provided in the form of FeCI 3 -6FI 2 0 and the iron (II) ions are provided in the form of FeCI 2 -4H 2 0. 16. A method according to any one of claims 13 to 15, wherein the organic solvent is miscible with the aqueous solution. 17. A method according to claim 16, wherein the organic solvent is acetone. 18. A method according to any one of claims 13 to 17, wherein the contacting step is conducted at a temperature that falls within a range of 70 Â°C to 85 Â°C. 19. A method according to any one of claims 13 to 18, further comprising the step of: washing the iron oxide particles capped with hydrophobic ligands on the surface there of with a mixture of acetone: methanol in a 1 :1 (vol/vol) ratio. 20. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the tertiary amine is a trialkylamine. 21. A method according to claim 20, wherein the trialkylamine is selected from the group consisting of triethylamine and trimethylamine. 22. A method according to claim 20, wherein the tertiary amine is triethylamine. 23. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the hydrophobic ligands are selected from the group consisting of an alkanoic acid, a saturated or unsaturated fatty acid, or a combination thereof. 24. A method according to claim 23, wherein the hydrophobic ligands are oleic acid. 25. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein the metal oxide particles are iron oxide particles. 26. A method according to claim 25, wherein the iron oxide particles are maghemite (y-Fe 2 0 3 ) and/or magnetite (Fe 3 0 4 ). 27. Metal oxide particles prepared according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 26. 28. Use of an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 26 as magnetic particles in a magnetism- mediated process selected from the group of processes consisting of magnetic hyperthermia treatment, magnetic labelling, magnetic separation, magnetic resonance imaging, magnetic particle imaging, magnetism-directed targeting and magnetism-induced heating. 29. A composition for use in a magnetism-mediated process comprising an aqueous dispersion of metal oxide particles produced according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 26, the magnetism-mediated process selected from the group of processes consisting of magnetic hyperthermia treatment, magnetic labelling, magnetic separation, magnetic resonance imaging, magnetic particle imaging, magnetism-directed targeting and magnetism- induced heating.
